channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: Smoke Free World A Possibility
item.description: A smoke-free world by 2040 is increasingly being viewed as an achievable public health goal rather than a distant ambition. New international research suggests that wider use of smoke-free nicotine products, including vapes and oral pouches, could significantly accelerate the decline in smoking rates. Countries such as Sweden, New Zealand and Japan are frequently cited as examples where access to reduced-risk alternatives has coincided with rapid falls in cigarette use. Critics of current policies argue that treating all nicotine products the same risks slowing progress and discouraging smokers from switching. The debate ultimately centres on whether harm reduction should play a central role in future tobacco control strategies. (Published on May 07, 2026byplanetofthevapes.co.uk)

item.title: Belgium to ban flavoured vapes from 2028 as crackdown intensifies
item.description: Belgium will ban flavored vapes starting September 1, 2028, to reduce youth vaping. Only tobacco or neutral flavors will be allowed, aiming to make vaping less attractive to young people. Minister Vandenbroucke highlighted the risks sweet flavors pose in attracting teens. With over a third of 15 to 20-year-olds in Belgium having tried vaping, removing flavors is seen as a way to decrease appeal. The decision aligns with advice from the Superior Health Council and is part of broader efforts to regulate vaping in the country. However, concerns exist about unintended consequences, enforcement challenges, and implications for adult smokers switching from traditional cigarettes. (Published on May 08, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: Can Ozempic Help You Quit Smoking? A Cardiologist Explains
item.description: Growing attention is being given to the possible connection between GLP-1 drugs such as Ozempic and smoking cessation. Discussion among medical professionals suggests that appetite- and craving-reducing treatments could potentially support people trying to move away from cigarettes and other nicotine products. According to some clinicians, this overlap may open new opportunities for combining harm reduction strategies with broader metabolic and cardiovascular care. At the same time, reluctance within parts of the pharmaceutical and medical sectors continues to limit wider exploration of nicotine harm reduction. The debate reflects a broader shift in how addiction, nicotine use and long-term public health challenges are increasingly being understood. (Published on May 08, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: How Many Jurisdictions Want Cannabis Pilot Trials In Germany?
item.description: Europe permits limited recreational cannabis commerce for research purposes through pilot trials, allowing local consumers, producers, and retailers to participate. Switzerland and the Netherlands have successful pilot projects, while Germany recently passed the CanG measure legalizing personal cannabis use and cultivation. Despite interest, Germany has not yet approved any pilot trial applications. Research aims to evaluate economic, health-related, and social impacts, potentially influencing broader legalization in Germany and the EU. Success in Switzerland and the Netherlands indicates Germany could also effectively implement pilot trials. (Published on May 08, 2026byinternationalcbc.com)

item.title: FDA authorizes fruit-flavored e-cigarettes for the first time
item.description: The FDA on Tuesday authorized the sale of fruit-flavored electronic cigarettes for the first time, clearing four products from Los Angeles-based vaping company Glas Inc. for adult use in the U.S. The authorized products — marketed under the names Gold, Sapphire, Classic Menthol, and Fresh Menthol — are e-liquid pods containing 50mg/ml of tobacco-derived nicotine, according to the FDA. Gold and Sapphire are mango and blueberry flavors, respectively, according to NBC News. Until Tuesday's action, every federally authorized vaping product had been either tobacco- or menthol-flavored. (Published on May 08, 2026byqz.com)
